name: browser
script_text:
# Lightpanda Browser - Engineering Overview

Lightpanda is an open-source headless browser built specifically for AI agents, automation, and web scraping. It executes JavaScript, supports partial Web APIs, and integrates with Playwright, Puppeteer, and chromedp via the Chrome DevTools Protocol.

The engineering standout: it runs nine times lighter on memory than Chrome and executes eleven times faster, with instant startup. This efficiency matters at scale—testing hundreds of pages becomes practical on modest infrastructure.

The browser handles DOM manipulation, event execution, and network interactions without the overhead of a full graphical interface. It's ideal for training data collection, automated testing, and agent-based web interaction where performance per compute dollar directly impacts viability.

name: stagehand
script_text:
# Stagehand YouTube Script

Stagehand is a production-ready framework for AI browser automation that balances code and natural language. Instead of choosing between low-level Selenium complexity or unpredictable high-level agents, developers can mix both approaches seamlessly.

The framework lets you write Playwright code for precise actions while using AI for navigating unfamiliar interfaces. Key features include action previewing, token-efficient caching, and one-line integration with OpenAI and Anthropic's computer use models.

Developers can execute individual actions, run larger agent tasks, and extract structured data from pages all within one framework. This makes stagehand ideal for building reliable, scalable browser automations in production environments.

name: playwright
script_text:
# Playwright Video Script

Playwright is a web testing and automation framework that controls Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit through a single API. This unified approach eliminates the need to maintain separate test suites for different browsers.

The framework delivers evergreen, reliable automation across Linux, macOS, and Windows. Developers can write tests once and execute them headlessly across all major browser engines. Installation is straightforward—either use the init command for a guided setup or manually install the test runner and browsers.

Playwright's primary strength lies in enabling comprehensive cross-browser testing without duplicating test logic.

name: steel-browser
script_text:
# Steel-browser Video Script

steel-browser is an open-source browser API designed for AI agents and applications that need to automate web interactions. It provides full Chrome control through Puppeteer and CDP protocols, handling session management, cookies, and local storage automatically. Developers get built-in proxy support, extension loading, anti-detection measures, and resource cleanup without managing infrastructure. The API converts pages to markdown, captures screenshots, and generates PDFs programmatically. With active development and a growing community, steel-browser eliminates the complexity of browser automation so teams can focus on AI logic instead of infrastructure. Perfect for building web agents that interact with live websites at scale.
